summary: Justin Brennan mentors Rory Lee on pitching a 28-unit townhome syndication deal (Millwood Townhomes in Kansas City) valued at $3.3M with a $1.025M equity raise. The session covers deal structure, underwriting validation, renovation strategy, and capital raising mechanics for a value-add multifamily property.
takeaways: Use LVT (luxury vinyl tile) glue-down flooring instead of LVP (luxury vinyl plank click-together) for easier unit-by-unit repairs without disrupting entire floors
Do not provide washers/dryers directly; instead use third-party appliance rental companies (like Appliance Warehouse) that handle maintenance to avoid burdening maintenance staff
Avoid demolishing old tile when installing new flooring; overlay with thin cement subfloor layer then glue down LVT to save time and cost
Keep operating expense ratios conservative at 35-40% for 1980s-era properties; validate assumptions in deal analyzer against property age guidelines
Design simple cosmetic renovations (paint, signage, lighting, stainless appliances, countertop refinishing) to drive rent growth without major capital expenditure
Exit cap rate projections should be equal to or higher than entry cap rate; conservatively underwrite at 6.6% exit when entering at 6.1% cap rate
Building local relationships with lenders, contractors, and brokers before deal sourcing saves ~1% loan origination fee versus using brokers to place loans

skill_candidates.change: Validate operating expense ratio against property age benchmarks in deal analyzer tool before finalizing underwriting
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin corrects Rory's 35% OpEx ratio for 1980 property, noting it should be 35-45% per guidelines, and demonstrates hovering over deal analyzer red arrows to reveal guidance thresholds

skill_candidates.change: Document conservative rent growth assumptions by gathering actual rental comps from competing properties within 1-2 mile radius
skill_candidates.evidence: Rory presents comps showing 1BR at $1,200-1,250 and 2BR at $1,400-1,425, then underwrites conservatively below those levels ($1,150 and $1,325)

skill_candidates.change: Establish local contractor, lender, and property manager relationships during market trip phase before sourcing deals to bypass broker fees
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin praises Rory for having local KC team pre-built, noting this saved 1% loan origination fee by dealing directly with community bank versus using loan broker

skill_candidates.change: Apply specific operational improvements (third-party washer/dryer rentals, LVT flooring, overlay subfloor technique) during renovation planning to reduce maintenance burden
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin teaches specific vendors (Appliance Warehouse) and flooring installation techniques (thin cement overlay vs demolition) that Rory immediately incorporates into project execution

title: Tuesday Mentorship Q&A - How to Handle Property Deals_05/19/26 | Justin Brennan
